response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with over 15 years of experience, but he fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, he has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — the job explicitly requires native-level Japanese as a mandatory qualification. Beyond that disqualifier, his background is overwhelmingly in web development, TypeScript/Node.js, and open-source tooling, with no discernible experience in embedded LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel development, or the required security and networking protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching). His C programming exposure is minimal and dated, confined to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience across multiple languages, platforms, and domains
Some low-level systems and embedded exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments and hardware development work at Altom Consulting
Strong Linux and DevOps familiarity through Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and GitLab administration
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency at any level — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this role
No experience with embedded networking security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF) or LAN-switch product development
No Linux kernel development, kernel vulnerability work, or chip-vendor SDK experience; primary stack is TypeScript and Node.js

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
